rulings.Case Title: PETER PEDRETTI et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Dana Companies, Llcs Notice Of Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 12, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Dana Companies, LLC's motion to dismiss for failure to prosecute pursuant to C.C.P. §§ 583.310, 583.360 is granted. From a review of the Register of Actions, the two remaining plaintiffs are Peter Pedretti and Paul Pedretti, Jr. As the Court sees no order relieving Brayton Purcell as counsel for him, Peter appears to still be represented by counsel and thus was given proper notice of this motion when it was e-served. Paul, representing himself, was given proper notice via mail service. No oppositions were filed. Hearing required unless defendant informs Peter Pedretti's counsel and Paul Pedretti, Jr. of this tentative ruling and they state that they does not wish to contest. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Defendant must bring a proposed order to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JACK SOWERS et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 12, 2015 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Timec Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is continued to June 25, 2015 pursuant to plaintiffs' C.C.P. § 437c(h) request, which is granted. Plaintiffs may file a supplemental opposition limited only to deposition testimony and a declaration from Mr. Clifton and an expert declaration. Such opposition is due by June 10, 2015. Supplemental reply due by June 17, 2015. If a hearing is requested to contest this continuance, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LEOLA BLACKMON-TATUM VS. GENERAL ELECTIC COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 12, 2015 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Dillingham Construction N.A., Inc.'s demurrer to the fifth and sixth causes of action in plaintiff's complaint for survival, wrongful death is overruled. Plaintiff's complaint states facts sufficient to state such causes of action against defendant as no facts in defendant's request for judicial notice demonstrate that either cause of action is time-barred. The fifth cause of action (Negligence II - Survival) meets the requirements of C.C.P. §§ 366.1 and 340.2(a). The sixth cause of action (Negligence II - Wrongful Death) meets the requirements of C.C.P. § 340.2(c). Defendant must answer within 10 days. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
